     The Numbers Business Report

Serenity (2005)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $25,514,517Details
International Box Office $14,804,923
Worldwide Box Office $40,319,440
Further financial details...

Synopsis

In this continuation of the television series “Firefly,” a group of rebels travels the outskirts of space aboard their ship, Serenity, outside the reach of the Alliance, a sinister regime that controls most of the universe. After the crew takes in Simon and his psychic sister, River, whom he has just rescued from Alliance forces, they find themselves being pursued by the Operative, an Alliance agent who will stop at nothing to find them.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$10,086,680 (39.5% of total gross)
Legs:2.53 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:63.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$39,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.0 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,188 opening theaters/2,189 max. theaters, 3.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $45,018,584

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 30th, 2005 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: November 10th, 2005 (Wide) (New Zealand)
September 15th, 2025 (Limited) (New Zealand)
Video Release: December 20th, 2005 by Universal Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for sequences of intense violence and action, and some sexual references.
Running Time: 119 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Visual Effects
Plot point: Cannibalism, Death of a Best Friend, Death of a Spouse or Fiancée / Fiancé, Fugitive / On the Run, Interracial Romance, Psychics
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Source:Based on TV
Genre:Action
Production Method:Animation/Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
